body.title: agent_datacenter: bus self-healing — calling device relaunches bus when unreachable
body.result: BusLauncher: agentctl init relaunch + 10s IMAP wait, auto-block after max_restart_failures. Router.send() catches ConnectionError/OSError, delegates to BusLauncher, retries once. bus_launcher=None disables healing (tests).
body.description:
If the bus (IMAP server / skeleton) is unreachable when a device tries to send, that device attempts to relaunch the bus automatically — unless the bus is blocked.

Proposed shape:
- In Router.send(): catch ConnectionError (IMAP server unreachable)
  If bus block flag is set: raise BusBlockedError (do not attempt relaunch)
  Otherwise: call BusLauncher.relaunch()
    BusLauncher: subprocess.Popen(['agentctl', 'start-skeleton']); wait up to 10s for IMAP to respond
    On relaunch success: retry send() once
    On relaunch failure after max_restart_failures: auto-block bus; raise BusUnavailableError
- Same restart-loop-protection semantics as other devices (max_failures, window_seconds from DeviceConfig)
- Logged: 'Bus unreachable; attempting relaunch (attempt N/M)'

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/bus/router.py (catch + relaunch in send())
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/bus/bus_launcher.py (new)

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: BusLauncher docstring explains self-healing vs manual restart semantics

Scope boundary:
IN: ConnectionError catch in Router.send(), BusLauncher.relaunch(), retry-once, auto-block on repeated failure
OUT: restart-loop-protection implementation (T-adc-restart-loop-protection covers that pattern generically)

Test plan:
- Mock IMAP down → Router.send() calls BusLauncher.relaunch()
- Mock relaunch succeeds → send() retries and succeeds
- Mock relaunch fails 3x → BusUnavailableError raised, bus auto-blocked
- Bus manually blocked → BusBlockedError raised immediately, no relaunch attempt
